vision: Modernize rail freight billing into a modular, scalable architecture that produces accurate charges and invoices consistently across all shipments.
Enable transparent, explainable billing (tariff selection, distance validation, surcharge and discount breakdown) with end-to-end auditability.
Support high-throughput billing cycles (including batch invoicing) using resilient services and well-defined boundaries without creating a tightly-coupled monolith.
problem_statements: Pricing outcomes are inconsistent when tariff lookup, distance validation, surcharges, and discounts are scattered across systems or implemented differently in multiple places.
Tariff records often contain incomplete or invalid distance bands; without strong validation, incorrect tariffs can be applied to shipments.
Batch invoicing is fragile when failures for one customer interrupt the full run; the process needs fault isolation and recoverability.

goals.text: Calculate freight charges using validated tariffs and distance bands with deterministic surcharge rules
Apply customer tier and regional discounts consistently with stored discount reasons for explainability
goals.metric: discount correctness rate and reason-code completeness

goals.text: Generate invoices that aggregate charges for a customer and billing date and compute gross, discount, and net totals
goals.metric: invoice generation latency and success rate

goals.text: Run batch invoice generation for ACTIVE customers with per-customer fault isolation and complete batch reporting
goals.metric: batch completion time and success ratio

goals.text: Persist charge components (base, surcharge, discount) for transparent invoice line-item breakdown
goals.metric: percentage of charges with full component breakdown

non_functionals.target: Charge calculation under 200ms p95 per shipment (excluding external distance lookup); invoice generation under 2s p95 per customer for typical volumes
Scale calculation and invoice services horizontally; batch invoicing supports thousands of customers with controlled parallelism
Idempotent operations for charge and invoice creation; retries with backoff; batch continues on per-customer failures
Deterministic pricing and discount rules with stored references (tariffId, reason codes) enabling reproducible outcomes
Encryption in transit and at rest; access control for tariff and billing operations; audit logging for pricing-affecting changes
Distributed tracing with correlation IDs, service metrics (tariff hit/miss, discount applied, invoice results) and structured logs

constraints: Tariff lookup must match origin, destination, and wagon type exactly; only tariffs whose distance band covers shipment distance may be used.
Surcharge is fixed at 5% of the base amount (base rate × distance).
Discount policy: Tier A = 10%, Tier B = 5%, Region WST = additional 2%; combine tier and regional discounts when both apply.
Invoices must be stored in currency INR with gross, discount, net, and discount reason.
Batch invoice generation must continue processing remaining customers even if individual customers fail.
assumptions: Shipment distance is available on the shipment record or can be resolved deterministically from origin and destination.
Tariff records contain required fields including base rate, currency, DIST-MIN, and DIST-MAX.
Customer records include tier (A/B) and region code (e.g., WST) and a status field (ACTIVE/INACTIVE).
Charge and invoice creation operations are idempotent to prevent duplicates during retries.

success_criteria.target: >= 99.9% discounts match tier/region policy with reason codes stored
>= 98% customers invoiced successfully per batch run with failures logged
100% charges store tariff reference and component breakdown; 100% invoices store gross/discount/net and discount reason

stories.title: Calculate Rail Freight Charges with Tariff Lookup
description: As a Billing Analyst, I want the system to automatically calculate rail freight charges based on tariff lookup so that charges are accurate and consistent across all shipments.
The ChargeCalculationService queries tariff storage using origin, destination, and wagon type and validates distance applicability using DIST-MIN/DIST-MAX.
If a valid tariff is found, it computes base = baseRate × distance and applies a fixed 5% surcharge, then persists a Charge with tariff reference.
acceptance_criteria: Given a shipment with origin, destination, and wagon type, when the ChargeCalculationService is called, then it should query the tariff database using these criteria.
Given a valid tariff is found with distance range covering the shipment distance, when calculating charges, then the system should multiply the base rate by distance and apply a 5% surcharge.
Given no applicable tariff is found for the route and wagon type, when calculating charges, then the system should return an error message 'NO_TARIFF_FOUND'.
Given a calculated charge, when the calculation is complete, then the system should create a Charge entity with gross amount, currency, and tariff reference.
Given distance validation is required, when checking tariff applicability, then the system should verify the shipment distance falls within the tariff's DIST-MIN and DIST-MAX range.

stories.title: Apply Customer Discounts to Charges
description: As a Billing Analyst, I want the system to automatically apply customer-specific discounts to charges so that pricing reflects customer tier and regional policies.
Discount rules: tier A = 10%, tier B = 5%, region WST = additional 2%. When multiple discounts apply, combine tier and regional discounts.
The system stores discount amount and a reason code on the charge for explainability.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer with tier 'A', when applying discounts, then the system should apply a 10% tier discount to the gross amount.
Given a customer with tier 'B', when applying discounts, then the system should apply a 5% tier discount to the gross amount.
Given a customer in the 'WST' region, when applying discounts, then the system should apply an additional 2% regional discount.
Given discount calculations are complete, when updating the charge, then the system should store both the discount amount and reason code.
Given multiple discounts apply, when calculating final discount, then the system should combine tier and regional discounts appropriately.

stories.title: Generate Individual Customer Invoice
description: As a Billing Analyst, I want to generate an invoice for a specific customer and date so that all charges for that period are consolidated into a single billing document.
The invoice aggregates all eligible charges, computes gross, applies customer discounts, and determines net amount.
Invoice persistence includes generating a unique invoice ID using customer ID and timestamp and storing currency as INR.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer ID and invoice date, when generating an invoice, then the system should aggregate all charges for shipments matching those criteria.
Given aggregated charges, when creating the invoice, then the system should calculate gross amount, apply customer discounts, and determine net amount.
Given invoice data is prepared, when persisting the invoice, then the system should generate a unique invoice ID using customer ID and timestamp.
Given a completed invoice, when saving to database, then the system should store invoice with currency 'INR', gross amount, discount amount, net amount, and discount reason.
Given invoice generation is successful, when returning results, then the system should provide the invoice ID and net amount to the calling process.

stories.title: Process Batch Invoice Generation for Active Customers
description: As a Billing Analyst, I want to run batch invoice generation for all active customers so that invoices are created efficiently for the billing cycle.
The batch process reads all customers with ACTIVE status, generates invoices per customer, captures results, and persists an InvoiceBatch summary.
Errors for individual customers are logged and do not stop the batch.
acceptance_criteria: Given a batch invoice process is initiated, when processing customers, then the system should read all customers with 'ACTIVE' status from the database.
Given an active customer record, when processing for invoice generation, then the system should call the invoice generation service for that customer.
Given invoice generation completes for a customer, when recording results, then the system should capture the invoice number, customer ID, and net amount.
Given batch processing is complete, when generating output, then the system should create an InvoiceBatch entity with batch metadata including start time, end time, and status.
Given any invoice generation fails, when handling errors, then the system should log the error but continue processing remaining customers.

stories.title: Calculate and Store Charge Components
description: As a Billing Analyst, I want the system to break down charges into components so that invoice line items show detailed charge breakdown for transparency.
For a calculated freight charge, create components for BASE and SURCHARGE; when discount is applied, create a DISCOUNT component with a negative amount.
All components must reference the parent Charge via ChargeId.
acceptance_criteria: Given a calculated freight charge, when creating charge components, then the system should create separate ChargeComponent records for base rate and surcharge.
Given a base rate calculation, when storing the component, then the system should create a ChargeComponent with type 'BASE', amount, and currency.
Given a 5% surcharge is applied, when storing the surcharge component, then the system should create a ChargeComponent with type 'SURCHARGE', calculated amount, and currency.
Given discount is applied, when storing discount information, then the system should create a ChargeComponent with type 'DISCOUNT', negative amount, and currency.
Given all components are created, when linking to parent charge, then each ChargeComponent should reference the parent Charge via ChargeId.

stories.title: Validate Tariff Data and Distance Ranges
description: As a Billing Analyst, I want the system to validate tariff data and distance ranges so that only applicable tariffs are used for charge calculations.
Tariff validation matches origin, destination, and wagon type exactly and selects tariffs only if shipment distance is within DIST-MIN and DIST-MAX.
Tariffs missing required fields (base rate or distance ranges) are rejected; if no valid tariff remains, return a validation error with details.
acceptance_criteria: Given a tariff lookup request, when searching for tariffs, then the system should match on origin, destination, and wagon type exactly.
Given multiple tariffs match the route criteria, when validating distance, then the system should select only tariffs where shipment distance falls within DIST-MIN and DIST-MAX.
Given distance validation fails for all tariffs, when no valid tariff is found, then the system should return a validation error with details.
Given tariff data is incomplete, when validating tariff records, then the system should reject tariffs missing required fields like base rate or distance ranges.
Given valid tariff is found, when proceeding with calculation, then the system should use the base rate from the validated tariff record.

style: Domain-driven design with bounded contexts (Tariff, Charge, Discount/Policy, Invoice, Batch Orchestration)
Synchronous request/response APIs for commands (calculate charge, apply discounts, generate invoice) with idempotency keys
Event-driven communication for downstream notifications and audit trails (e.g., charge calculated, invoice generated)
Database-per-service with well-defined ownership; avoid cross-service writes; use API composition where needed
Resilient patterns: retries with backoff, circuit breakers, outbox/inbox for reliable event publishing and consumption
Batch orchestration with parallel workers, per-customer fault isolation, and resumable runs
tech_stack: API gateway for routing, auth, throttling (Kong / NGINX / cloud APIM equivalents)
Microservices runtime (FastAPI / Spring Boot / .NET) with containerization (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes)
Service-owned datastores: PostgreSQL for tariffs/invoices and a service-appropriate store for others (PostgreSQL/MongoDB), depending on access patterns
Message broker for asynchronous events (RabbitMQ or Kafka)
Observability: OpenTelemetry tracing, centralized logs, metrics (Prometheus/Grafana or cloud equivalents)
Config and secrets management (Vault / AWS SSM / Azure Key Vault) and CI/CD pipelines
